results.title: GetMint Alternatives: Top Competitors Compared - GetMint
results.content:
## Copied to clipboardTop GetMint Alternatives In-Depth

The alternatives below span three categories: specialist GEO tools (Profound, Peec, Scrunch), traditional SEO suites with GEO modules (Semrush, BrightEdge), and execution-led platforms (Writesonic). Review scarcity across all options reflects market maturity, not quality signals.

### Copied to clipboardProfound

Profound positions as the enterprise-grade specialist for organizations prioritizing analytics depth over execution features. Its differentiation centers on proprietary conversation data access: 120M+ conversation dataset revealing actual user intent and query patterns, not synthetic prompt testing.

#### Copied to clipboardOverview [...] For startups and SMEs (budget-first): Start with Peec AI ($103/month) for affordable monitoring with unlimited team seats, or Writesonic ($49/month) if content generation matters more than analytics depth. Graduate to Scrunch ($250/month) or Profound ($399/month) as AI visibility becomes strategic priority.
 For mid-market B2B SaaS (feature-balanced): Scrunch ($250/month) offers the strongest fit for organizations with multi-persona buying committees. The persona segmentation directly addresses B2B complexity that aggregate tools miss. Profound ($399/month) is the alternative if analytics depth and conversation data access matter more than persona tracking. [...] ### Copied to clipboardWritesonic

Writesonic represents the execution-led alternative: combining visibility monitoring with AI content generation for teams wanting integrated creation and tracking.

#### Copied to clipboardOverview

Unlike monitoring-focused tools, Writesonic positions GEO as part of broader AI content workflow. For teams frustrated by tools that show visibility gaps without helping create optimized content, this integrated approach reduces tool-switching and addresses the actionability gap that some platforms leave.

#### Copied to clipboardQuick Facts

results.title: 5 Best Scrunch AI Alternatives for 2026: Move From Diagnosis to Execution - GetMint
results.content:
### 1. GetMint – Best for Content Creation and Agility

GetMint is the best choice for content teams that need to fix visibility gaps immediately without worrying about complex infrastructure integrations.

GetMint's AI visibility overview dashboard

Scrunch AI fixes how bots read your code via the Agent Experience Platform (AXP), whereas GetMint helps you fix what the AIs actually say about you.

If you’re a content lead, you’ll quickly find out that Scrunch AI identifies the gap (e.g., “Update your pricing page”) but leaves the drafting to you. GetMint bridges this gap with an integrated Content Studio. [...] ### 

### 3. What Is Your Volume Requirement?

If you’re dealing with high volume (500+ prompts), Scrunch AI might be the more cost-efficient option. Their Growth plan ($500/mo) offers 700 prompts, which is a better cost-per-prompt ratio than most competitors.

If you only need to track your money terms rather than the entire long-tail, paying $500/mo for Scrunch is a wasted budget. GetMint or Semrush are the better alternatives\\.\\

## 

## Stop Overengineering GEO Today

Scrunch AI is a powerful piece of infrastructure, but it treats GEO as an engineering problem. For most marketing teams, GEO is a content problem.

You don’t need to intercept bots at the CDN level to win a citation in ChatGPT. You just need to provide the best, most authoritative answer. [...] If your site works fine but the AI just doesn’t like your brand, you need a GEO tool with strong content creation capabilities. GetMint and AthenaHQ are better choices because they focus on rewriting the text to win the citation.

### 

### 2. Who Is Running the Platform?

If your developers or technical SEOs are running the platform, pick Hall AI. They’ll appreciate the raw logs, API access, and real-time bot forensics.

If your content or brand lead is using the tool, choose GetMint. They need a workspace to generate briefs and fix sentiment issues, not a log of crawler behavior.

If your VP of communications is the primary user, choose Profound. They need high-level sentiment tracking and historical data to report to the board.

### 

### 3. What Is Your Volume Requirement?

results.title: The 5 best Mint alternatives to replace the budgeting app that shut down
results.content:
So I set out to find the best Mint alternatives in the wake of its disappointing demise. I gave Credit Karma, Intuit's other financial app, a try but found it to be a poor Mint substitute. The following guide lays out my experience testing some of the most popular Mint replacement apps available today in search of my next budgeting app.

Our pick for best Mint alternative remains Quicken Simplifi, even months after the Mint shutting down, thanks to its easy to use app, good income and bill detection and its affordable price. But there are plenty of other solid options out there for those with different needs. If you're also on the hunt for a budgeting app to replace Mint, we hope these details can help you decide which of the best budgeting apps out there will be right for you. [...] Monarch Money uses a combination of networks to connect with banks, including Plaid, MX and Finicity, a competing network owned by Mastercard. (I have a quick explainer on Plaid, the industry standard in this space, toward the end of this guide.) As part of an update in late December, Monarch has also made it easier to connect through those other two networks, if for some reason Plaid fails. Similar to NerdWallet, I found myself completing two-factor authentication every time I wanted to get past the Plaid screen to add another account. Notably, Monarch is the only other app I tested that allows you to grant access to someone else in your family — likely a spouse or financial advisor. Monarch also has a Chrome extension for importing from Mint, though really this is just a shortcut for [...] YNAB

### Mint alternative – honorable mention

#### YNAB

$110 a year or $15 a month$99 a year or $14.99 a month

Monthly cost: Starts at $8 | Tracks spending: Yes | Investment tracking: Yes | Links to bank accounts: Yes | Mobile app: iOS, Android

An app with a cult following that promotes a different, more hands-on approach to budgeting. Not for people who want a set-and-forget experience.

Pros

 Particularly strong emphasis on budgeting
 Unique "zero-dollar" approach to financial planning that some people swear by
 

Cons

 Steep learning curve
 Harder to use certain features on the mobile app than on the web

See at YNAB

results.title: 8 Best Mint Budgeting App Alternatives (Free & Paid) – ROB BERGER
results.content:
Best for: Budget, subscription management, and investment tracking

Try Monarch Money

On Monarch’s Website

## 8 Best Mint Alternatives

### 1. Monarch Money–Best Overall Budgeting App

I’ve used and tested Monarch for about two years, and it’s an excellent budgeting app and alternative to Mint for several reasons.

First, its user interface is beautiful and informative. The dashboard gives you quick access to your most important budget details. These details include recent transactions, a comparison of income and expenses for the current month, and your net worth. If you track goals, such as saving for emergencies, the dashboard shows your progress.

Fun Fact: Monarch’s co-founder and CEO, Val Agostino, was the first product manager on the original team that built Mint.com. [...] In evaluating these apps, I focused on the following key criteria:

 Cost
 Ease of use
 Mobile app availability
 Ability to consolidate all accounts in one place
 Budgeting tools
 Investment tracking tools

## Mint Alternatives FAQs

### What is the best free alternative to Mint?

Empower is the best free Mint replacement. It comes with nearly every feature Mint offers and many additional tools. It’s particularly well suited for those who want to manage all of their money in one place, including investments.

### What is the best option to migrate Mint data to the new app?

Most modern budgeting apps support importing CSV exports from Mint. Monarch Money and Quicken Simplifi both have CSV import features, though you may need to re-categorize some transactions. [...] ### What is the best Mint replacement for calendar budgeting?

Monarch Money offers a strong calendar view of your budget, making it a good option if calendar-based budgeting is your priority.

### Which Mint alternatives allow you to schedule bill payments?

With both Tiller Money and YNAB, you can plan future bill payments. Tiller offers a Bill Payment Tracker template and YNAB enables you to allocate funds to bills you plan to pay in the future.

Whatever tool you choose, the key is to pick one that works for you. For me, that’s Monarch Money. One or more of the above Mint alternatives, however, should suit the needs of most looking to better manage their money.
